meta.title:
Operational control
shouldn't break as you scale.
meta.deck: AIROPS centralizes operations, pilots, aircraft, documentation and validation into one structured workflow for drone operators.

problem.title: As operations grow, fragmentation becomes risk.
problem.intro: Watofly is built for drone operators that need one place to plan, execute, control and audit their operations. As fleets, pilots and missions grow, critical information starts spreading across people, folders and disconnected systems.

center.title: The gap
center.body: Distributed information creates operational risk: inconsistent execution, weak auditability, duplicated work and less control over who flew what, when and under which conditions.
center.metric: Growth without structure creates safety risk

mvp.title: A usable MVP, built for real operations.
mvp.intro: This phase is not about building the entire platform from day one. It is about building the first usable version of AIROPS: one that operators can actually use, validate and improve through real workflows.

items.title: Validate real product usage
items.body: Put the product in front of operators early and learn from actual operational behaviour, not assumptions.

items.title: Centralize the operational core
items.body: Bring operations, users, aircraft and documentation into one controlled system of record.

items.title: Reduce operational error
items.body: Replace ad-hoc execution with structured flows, checklists and role-based actions.

items.title: Lay the foundation to scale
items.body: Build the first version on a modular architecture prepared for future integrations and product growth.

flow.title: One operational flow, end to end.
flow.intro: AIROPS connects planning, execution and validation in one operational chain. Every relevant action stays structured, visible and traceable from creation to closure.

steps.title: Login
steps.body: The system identifies company and role from the first access.

steps.title: Setup
steps.body: Admins create pilots, aircraft and the minimum structure required to operate.

steps.title: Create operation
steps.body: Operations are planned with date, pilot, aircraft, location and notes.

steps.title: Execute
steps.body: Pilots access their assigned work from a personal operational view.

steps.title: Checklist
steps.body: Start and closing checklists create operational traceability at the right moment.

steps.title: Upload evidence
steps.body: Photos, videos and documents are attached to the operation file in Google Drive.

steps.title: Validate
steps.body: Admins review data and documentation, then validate or flag issues.

steps.title: Review
steps.body: Teams filter and consult operations through a minimal operational control layer.

role.title: What Tailor is responsible for.
role.intro: Tailor is not only building screens. We are defining how the operational core of the product should behave across architecture, workflows, safety and future evolution.

items.title: Architecture and security
items.body: Multi-company cloud setup, authentication, sessions, roles and protection of critical operational data.

items.title: Operational core design
items.body: Data model and workflow logic for users, pilots, UAS, operations and documentation.

items.title: UX for real field operations
items.body: Clear interfaces designed for tablets and iPads, reducing friction in critical day-to-day actions.

items.title: Scalability and future evolution
items.body: Modular codebase prepared for future integrations, product growth and more advanced operational capabilities.

security.title: Built for operational trust.
security.intro: Security is part of the product definition from the beginning. AIROPS needs to protect sensitive operational data while keeping execution practical for the teams that use it every day.

columns.title: Platform security
points: Audit trail of operations and changes

columns.title: What Tailor brings
points: Architecture designed for critical operational use

stack.title: A stack chosen for control and future evolution.
stack.intro: The proposed stack favors maintainability, traceability and predictable evolution over unnecessary complexity.

items.title: Frontend
items.body: Modern JavaScript frontend with modular architecture, responsive UI and reusable components for forms, tables and dashboards.

items.title: Backend
items.body: Node.js API with secure role management, session handling and business logic kept separate from the interface layer.

items.title: Database
items.body: MySQL relational model designed for traceability, auditability and operational history.

items.title: Infrastructure
items.body: Cloud deployment with separated environments, secure secrets management and horizontal scalability as the product grows.

roadmap.title: From definition to go-live in 9 weeks.
roadmap.intro: The roadmap is designed to get a first usable version of AIROPS into production quickly, while keeping enough structure to support future iterations.

weeks.label: Week 1
weeks.title: Definition, design and architecture
weeks.objective: Set the foundations of the product.
items: Technical architecture for multi-company access and roles

weeks.label: Week 2
weeks.title: Core users, roles and security
items: Role system for admin and user

weeks.label: Week 3
weeks.title: Operations core
weeks.objective: Define the heart of the product.

weeks.label: Week 4
weeks.title: Operational calendar
items: Calendar view by day and week

team.title: A lean team built for speed and ownership.
intro: The team is intentionally compact: enough to move quickly, with clear accountability across product, backend, frontend and design.
This setup is designed to define, build and launch the first operational version of AIROPS without overbuilding the organization around it.

investment.title: The investment.
investment.intro: A focused team, a defined scope and a first operational release in 9 weeks.
investment.note: This budget covers the team required to define, build and launch the first usable version of AIROPS, with enough depth to validate real usage and enough structure to support future iterations.

contact.title: Next step.
contact.body: A working session to align scope, priorities and operational assumptions before kickoff.
contact.name: Diego Méndez
